title: LexonElec Custom Coiled Keyboard Cable, 2.0M(0.66ft) USB-C to USB-A TPU Mechanical Keyboard Cable, Detachable Metal Aviator Double-Sleeved Wire for Gaming Keyboard(Orange)
features: 【High Quality and Durable】LexonElec custom coiled keyboard cable Uses four high-quality copper wires and aluminum shielding foil as the core, surface adopts metal and TPU. Professional reverse coil, strong and durable, not afraid of bending and will not break.

【Metal Aviation Connector】The aviator connector allows you to quickly attach different cables to your keyboard or other accessories. Screw-type plug-in lock, self-locking structure, strong connection and stable transmission. All-steel metal, full of texture, Enhance the keyboard experience.
【Professional reversed Coil】Coiled shape, more substantial, not easy to tangle. Even if it is overstretched, it will not only not be damaged but will quickly return to its original shape.

【High-speed Charging and Transfer】USB Type-C to USB-A cable supports safe and fast data synchronization and charging, USB cable is compatible with most USB C mechanical gaming keyboards or printers, PS4, Switch.

【Enough length】The length of the cable exceeds similar products on the market! 7.9 inches long with 20 mm Inner diameter coiled cable+1.1 meter straight cable. This length is enough to meet the distance of all keyboards. It will be a perfect replacement for your OEM or original cables with superior performance.

【Quality Assurance】 After a number of technical tests, including transmission stability, sturdy cables durability, resilience of coils, firm welding of the joints, heavy load test on connect and swing test. Each product you own is high -quality.

reviews.title: Very nice coiled keyboard cable.
reviews.text:
The cable came nicely coiled with no flaws. Plugs are high quality and the screw connection type is pretty heavy duty I don't see this breaking. Only wished the straight part of the cable was longer.

I highly recommend this cable for anyone looking for a custom looking cable that wont break the bank.

reviews.title: Works as advertised!
reviews.text: This cable worked exactly as advertised. My keyboard has a USB c connector, and it fit together perfectly. I love the look of this cable as it neatly tucks under my desk and doesn't leave any unnecessary excess hanging around. Overall very happy with this product!

reviews.title: Decent aviator cable for my keyboard setup.
reviews.text: These cables are an aesthetically great match for my “rebel scum” keyboard build.  The connections are tight and the materials used seems like they are great quality.  I don’t really have any complaints about the quality of this product.  I think it’s important to note these are not braided cables, and have a soft rubber feel, much like you would get on any standard USB type cable.

reviews.title: Good Quality!
reviews.text: Bright white coiled cable makes my desk look neater and organized. Matches my desk decor better. Keyboard response is the same as the original cable it came with, no complaints here.

reviews.title: Treat your keyboard to a nicer cable
reviews.text: What a nice cable, with solid metal USB plugs, a clean aviator connector, and tight and even coils. I got it in purple, to match some keycaps I like, and it looks perfect. The USB-C plug is about 5.5mm thick, so should fit most recessed plugs. I was able to charge every device I tried, even a chromebook (lenovo duet) and phone (pixel 6a).

title: PWNAGE Ultra Custom Gaming Keyboard - Full Size RGB - USB C Mechanical Keyboard Hot Swappable Switches & PBT Keycaps - Black Metal Top Plate - Linear Red Switches
features: Full Size Ultrafast Optical Mechanical Switches
Over 115,168,768 possible colorway and switch combination options
Hot Swappable PBT Keycaps
Hot Swappable Gateron Optical Switches
Dampening Foam

reviews.title: Nice manual keyboard with a lighter touch.
reviews.text:
This review is for the Pwnage Ultra Custom Mechanical Keyboard: Gateron Optical Switches.

The Package was delivered quickly in perfect condition. In the box was the full-size white keyboard, detachable USB cord, keycap remover, and manual.

Setup was plug and play, no additional drivers needed for Windows 11. The keyboard replaced my 105 key Cherry Blue switch manual black keyboard... which has worked very well with just basic cleaning for 5 years. Compared to my old keyboard this keyboard adds multi color, and some very intense graphics, should you choose to use them... I see videos already posted for this product cycling thru the graphics available so I will just show pictures of the static color I have chosen.

The Red Optical/Manual switches on this keyboard are a little quieter than the blue cherry switches I am used to – but they are by no means quiet…it definitely has the tap, tap, tap sound inherent in all manual keyboards The press seems very easy... much lighter and easier than the blue manual switches I am used to. And so far, I think I prefer the red optical/manual switches on this keyboard. Of course, typing is very subjective to taste and your mileage may vary.

Overall, I can honestly say that I approve of this keyboard, and will keep this one as my everyday keyboard, unless, of course my wife steals it for her home office workstation… she has after all been eyeballing it.

reviews.title: Overall an excellent full size keyboard
reviews.text:
Unboxing was fun. Nicely packaged. Keyboard is nice and large, a little too large for my tastes coming from someone who uses a 65%. But good for every day use and office use with a little side of gaming in the evening. Switches are nice and the RGb effects are plentiful. I think being able to switch the plates easily for those who want a quick easy solution to being able to completely change their keyboard without having to spend the time and money to build a completely custom one one.

Definitely a great full size.

reviews.title: Gaming all the way, I could not use this for extended typing... loudness lol
reviews.text:
This keyboard is very well made, has good heft and stability to it. The keyboard includes a braded USB-C and a key cap remover tool. The keys feel really good when gaming, the input is instantaneous and the feel and travel is really smooth. The only downside for me it is that it is very loud. I use this when my wife is around and she is immediately annoyed, lol, so I only use it when I game on my own. That being said, this is my favorite keyboard. I love all the RGB patterns, (check video). I love the feel of the keys, when I game I feel that is just the right travel distance and feel for my taste. The rear pop out legs help me put this bad boy at the right angle. The finish is really even and dark, the keys are not your typical white on black, it is more of a clear or light grey, this makes the contrast a little harder to see what key is what, so if you don't type well and need to reference the keyboard visually it might be a harder to see. All in all this is a very good entry level keyboard and it is very budget friendly.

+ RGB Great patterns
+ Hefty and stable
+ Silky smooth keys
+ USB-C braded cable
+ Mac / Win compatible
+ Black finish is matte and clean
- Loud Keys

Hope this was helpful!

reviews.title: Beautiful Keyboard
reviews.text:
I was first impressed with this keyboard by the packaging. The flap that secures the box top it held closed by magnets. Unique and impressive.

As to the keyboard, it is beautifully white and has top-of-the-line red key actuators. With these you get a definite "click" when you hit the keys informing you the character has been chosen. The keytops supplied are also all white and they supply a keytop pulling tool, obviously expecting you to switch out some of the keytops to suit your taste, making this superior quality unit fully customized to your taste.

A premium keyboard for those who appreciate true quality in a computer input instrument.

reviews.title: Great and beautiful keyboard
reviews.text: [[VIDEOID:784296779c5343262f2f9394390eabd8]] First of all, the packaging box of this keyboard caught my eye. The box was attracted by magnets, which is novelty for me. The keyboard is beautiful, I like the white keyboard because it looks very clean, and there are many kinds of RGB lights, which means that we have many options. It is very comfortable to press the keys and has a good sense of hitting. Meanwhile, the keyboard is a little louder for me. I don't feel it when I play games, but I feel a little noisy when I work, and I'm afraid it will disturb my roommates because our desktops are in the same room, but the sound is within the acceptable range. Overall, I am satisfied with this beautiful and useful keyboard.

title: SteelSeries PRISMCAPS - Double Shot Pudding-Style Keycaps - Durable PBT Thermoplastic - Compatible with a Wide Range of Mechanical Keyboards - White
description: Built for extreme durability with thermoplastic PBT keycaps and with enhanced RGB pudding-style lighting, PrismCaps include 2 additional sets of bottom row keycaps, making them compatible with the widest range of MX stem mechanical keyboards.
features: Ultra-wide compatibility with both standard and non-standard bottom row keycap sets to fit most mechanical keyboard brands, including SteelSeries, Razer, Corsair, and virtually all other MX stem mechanical keyboards
Standard US ANSI 104 keys works on full sized, tenkeyless, and 60% mini keyboard layouts
Enhanced lighting displays brilliant pudding-style RGB illumination through a specially engineered double shot design with translucent sides and letters
Retains consistent texture and appearance even after heavy use with highly durable double shot PBT keycaps
Included keycap puller is built with finger hole grip to pull any keycap out with ease and flexible enough to fit around any standard keycap.

reviews.title: for apex pro
reviews.text: bought these for my apex pro. they fit perfect and i definitely like them better than the caps that came with the keyboard

reviews.title: Great keys for $25
reviews.text: I really like these keys because it is very nicely built. The plastic is thick and there is some texture. The only problem with the texture part is that the A,W,S,D buttons are going to wear down and it will smooth out. I would appreciate if they can do something to make it more durable. I’d still recommend this for putty caps.

reviews.title: Great keycaps
reviews.text: i got these for my Apex Pro they look amazing on it about the only thing about them is more about the apex then the keycaps,the space bar only has one led on the keyboard in the center so does not light up the spacebar as much as the rest of the keys and is pretty noticeable...the kit came with everything needed to be used on several different keyboards that i have so i might get another set for my corsair boards next.

reviews.title: They look so good!
reviews.text: Made my Steelseries Apex 7 keyboard pop even more. They feel great with my Red Linear Switches. They are a little more expensive than some other brands, but I feel like it was better to go with the same brand since I have seen other people have issues with switching out the keycaps on Steelseries keyboards.

reviews.title: Love these!
reviews.text: Amazing PBT pudding keycaps, I absolutely love these! I chose these to match my SteelSeries keyboard and I'm very happy with them.

title: NPKC Black Gray Mixed Dolch Thick PBT 104 87 61 Keycaps OEM Profile Key caps for MX Mechanical Keyboard (Only Keycap) (87 Side Print)
description: This link is only the keycaps,no keyboard included.The keycap loading board is only to take photo,not included in the sold package.We will pack the keycaps in our own carton. This blank keycap set is no lengend.The printed ones are laser engraved.You can customize your own font and only need to add usd5 for your own font.Nearly all font is ok if can be opened on AI software. The keycap is made of durable PBT material,the thickness is about 1.5mm. Compatiability It can be used for most Cherry MX Gateron MX Kailh MX and all other MX switches keyboard. Incompatiability If the spacebar is 6u 6.5u 7u instead of 6.25u,it can't be compatible,such as some HHKB Cosair .You can order additional keys needed.We will produce special full set for them soon. Some old Cherry keyboard whose Spacebar Capslock back hole is different is not compatible.We have cherry 117 121 keyset owns both special Space and Capslock in other link. Some Costar stabilizer keyboard whose spacebar may be clicky for thick caps.You can adjust the wire or cut the edges of inner spacebar.This is a easy problem.If you have met this problem,just leave us message.We will help you solve it. After Service If some keys missing or broken on the way,just leave us message,we will send you missing or broken ones soon.Don't worry about it and don't leave feedback immediately.

This keycap is made of 90% PBT.The thickness is around 1.5mm.The printed one is laser etched.The blank one is no legend
This keycap is compatible with most standard ANSI MX switches keyboard whose bottom modifier key is 1x6.25u 7x1.25u.Space is around 11.7cm.
This YMDK keycap is not compatible with Corsair Razer Keyboard..64 68 75 84 96 keyboard can't use it as well

reviews.title: Returned
reviews.text: Returned

reviews.title: Perfect
reviews.text: Fit perfectly and feel amazing. Great printing too.

title: Akko Keycaps Top Printed Shine-Through with ASA Profile, White Keycaps PBT Double-Shot Keycaps for Mechanical Keyboards
features: SHINE-THROUGH KEYCAPS – Deliver premium and shining RGB experiences with Akko ASA Shine-through keycap sets! Come with extremely durable and resistant double-shot Shine-through keycaps, the legend shine through keycaps brings unique and great RGB effects.

ULTIMATE CUSTOMIZATION FLEXIBILITY – Comes with Akko ASA profile, the keycap set is suitable for a variety of layouts including 61-key(60%), 64-key, 68-key (65%), 84-key (75%), 87-key (TKL), 98-Key (Classic 1800), 100-Key (96%), Alice and full 108-key size, and thus offers strong flexibility for keyboard personalization.

ASA PROFILE – Akko’s proprietary ASA profile is tailored to offer a more familiar and comfortable typing experience by matching the height for OEM profile while maintaining the aesthetics and spheric top of the classic SA profile.
PBT DOUBLE-SHOT – Double-shot keycaps are extremely durable and resistant to wear even for decades, and the legends are particularly vibrant, sharp with high contrast.

FOR MX STRUCTURE SWITCHES – Compatible with MX structure mechanical switches and mainstream keyboards including but not limited to Cherry, Gateron, Akko and Kailh. Large keys of non-standard layout & stabilizers may not be compatible.

reviews.title: Not the brightest shine-through, but for the material and price it's worth it.
reviews.text:
Akko's ASA profile is a taller profile kind of like SA. Feels great to type on and is fine for gaming if you don't mind the extra travel time on each keystroke. The finish on these has a great texture and it's PBT so it shouldn't wear out quickly. "Shouldn't" being relative since I tend to shred keycaps as though my fingers were first dipped in acid before typing.

30.00 for keycaps of this quality and for the number of keys is great especially in a hobby where everything is over priced, over hyped, and overly bogged down in group buys and pre-orders that take literal years to fulfill.

reviews.title: Shine-Through keycaps, look amazing
reviews.text: These shine-through keycaps look awesome.  They are super easy to install, like most keycaps.  It is just a bit time consuming.  I love being able to change out keys and switches and seem to collect keycaps.  These are great and sound and feel amazing.

🧡WARM TIMPS-Keycaps only, the keyboard in the picture is only for the demo and is not for sale. ANSI, US layout. This set cannot be used for 64/68/75/84/96 layout which need special keys such as “Shift”，“Alt” etc.

reviews.title: Great Keycaps for the price!!
reviews.text: When these keycaps came in I wasn't expecting a whole lot because of the price but surprisingly they're actually pretty nice. Usually on cheaper sets like this the legending and print quality can vary quite a bit but these keycaps were really consistent and is on par with some other sets I've had that cost twice as much so that was nice to see. Overall they're pretty great for the price and I honestly recommend them if you if you're looking for "designer" style keycaps.

reviews.title: Beautiful keycaps for designers!
reviews.text: These keycaps are beautiful! I use photoshop often so I already have the shortcuts memorized but having these keycaps are just so nice regardless. They feel really nice and thick, the printing on them is nice and clear, and I love the color way for them. I dont use this board often even when working in photoshop and really just use this board and keycap set as a decorative piece to add to my collection of keyboards. I love these keycaps!!

reviews.title: Looks like durgod k320
reviews.text: Awesome caps.  Looks exactly lie durgod k320

reviews.title: Very sturdy product. Was as described
reviews.text: I bought it as a gift for my grandson. Just what he wanted. Working good

reviews.title: Good Keycap Set! Beat Expectations
reviews.text:
I had initially bought this keycap set as a placeholder for my GMK Samurai Keycap set that I was set to receive. However, I had learned that Drop would ship them in late July, and since I was unsure if I was getting a refund, I bought this keycap set as a placeholder.

This keycap set broke all expectations that I held for cheap keycaps. They feel to be of good quality, with a high-ish thock sound that is what I wanted. Overall, it is a good keycap set that I would recommend to people if they are looking for good quality and good-sounding keycaps.

Also, I did get a refund for the GMK keycaps so I will be sticking with these keycaps, although they don't match as they were not meant to pair together and were rather a last-minute decision.

reviews.title: Cumple las espectativas

reviews.title: Best keycaps I have ever used.
reviews.text:
Bought these for my GMMK pro.

I have owned several mechanical keyboards, but never bought a high-end set of keycaps. Unlike every other set I've ever used, these just seem to ignore dirt and grease. I can stuff my face with a big Mac and go straight to typing, and the caps stay completely clean. I don't know how that works but I won't question it.

Other keyboards require weekly wipe downs, I have never had to do it with these caps.

They're also noticeably built well. These are going to outlast my fingers by a hundred years, likely.

reviews.title: Legend doesn’t glow like other pics
reviews.text: For my Tofu65, I do like these pudding key caps, but I’m not sure why the legends on my key caps aren’t transparent. Everyone who posted reviews with pics show their legends glowing, so I’m not sure why mine are different. My other issue is I got several duplicative keys, and I’m missing a few. For example, I only have one home key and about 4 page down keys. So that’s slightly annoying. I’ve seen some others complain about this similar thing as well. But the feel and look of these key caps are quality. Slightly disappointed.

reviews.title: Great caps work as advertised
reviews.text: Works great

title: Vaydeer 4 Fully Programmable Keys with Floating Window and Macro Multifunctional Keypad, One-Handed Mechanical Keyboard Support NKRO, Hotkeys, One-Click Start, Compatible with iOS,Windows
features: 🖥 User-Friendly Interface and Floating Window：With user-friendly interface and real-time floating window, you will never forget the function of the key being used at the moment. This one handed macro mechanical keyboard can make your work faster and more efficient, and make the game experience more comfortable and smooth. Besides, you can carry the macro keyboard anywhere due to the compact and elegant design.

🖥 OTA Upgrade and Setting Sharing：The macro keyboard supports OTA online upgrade. Timely push message reminds you to update the firmware for more useful functions. Easy setting and you can export/import your settings for backup. No more set up for different computers. You can also share your settings with friends. If you have any problems with this one-handed macro mechanical keyboard, please feel free to contact us, we are sure to provide you with a satisfactory solution.

reviews.title: Hey, this is nifty
reviews.text:
JUST GOT THIS LAST NIGHT -- CHECK MY PURCHASE DATE -- AND IT WORKS! FEW MINUTES TO UNDERSTAND THE APP AND CONFIGURATION BUT NOW I'M UP AND FLYING.
USING BUTTONS FOR TRANSCRIBING SHORTCUTS; HIGHLIGHTING TEXT, BOLD, UNDERLINE, LARGER, DELETE, PLAY/PAUSE, FORWARD AND REWIND AUDIO/VIDEO TRACKS.
9-KEY *DOES* WORK THE GLOBAL APPLE MEDIA KEYS -- PLAY/PAUSE, REV, FFW AND IT WILL SCRUB AUDIO/VIDEO AS NORMAL AKA HOLDING DOWN THE REWIND KEY IN QUICKTIME, FOR EXAMPLE.
THE DEPRESSING OF KEYS ARE OLD SCHOOL -- LONG THROW AND LOUD -- BUT WILL GET USED TO.
CONTOUR SHUTTLE PRO V2 WILL ALSO GET AN AUDITION.
SUPER HAPPY WITH THIS LITTLE CONTROLLER, IT'S A TIDY SQUARE ON THE TABLE, AS WELL. LONG CABLE LEAD.

reviews.title: Awesome keypad for Day Trading Hotkeys
reviews.text: This keypad is good quality. Keys are tactile and clicky. Board is solid and no flex. Cable is braided and good quality. Very happy with this device. The programming can be flashed to the hardware so you do NOT need to have the software running for this to work.

reviews.title: I live the macro board
reviews.text: I am a software developer and use this everyday. It was easy to program and I love the onscreen UI that tells me what I have mapped to each button.  I also like the ability to store multiple button sets in other “presets” on the board. Overall it works great and I am glad I picked this up.

reviews.title: very good for the price
reviews.text:
I got this for a few different use such as photoshop, gaming, and other work software.
At first, looking at the photo I thought it was a metal case, but it's just a thin metal on the top and everything is plastic. Therefore, it's really light, and when you tap the keys it sounds so hallow, so I end up opening up taping the board, and filling it with some foam. This made a bit better but not much different. As for the software it is easy to use and there are a few layers (profile) settings that you can set it up for different software if needed. Also, the software is very easy to use.

Overall for the price, it is a good macro keypad for many different uses.

reviews.title: Nice Additional Keys
reviews.text: The software couldn’t be any easier to program. Keys have a nice feel to them. You can program up to 6 layers with this software. I use it with my CNC CAD/CAM software for repetitive functions that I use most. I would buy it again.

title: 4-Port HDMI KVM Switch – 4K 60Hz – QHD 144Hz - Audio Output and USB Sharing – Mechanical & Gaming Keyboards Supported – HKS0401A2U
description:
This 4x1 HDMI KVM Switch allows you to share one set of “keyboard, video monitor, and mouse” (“KVM”) with up to four PC’s or video devices (Xbox, PS4, Firestick, etc). The switcher does not require software and works with Windows, MacOS, Unix/Linux, Raspberri Pi, etc. HDMI 2.0 protocol supports 4K@60hz and blazing 144hz@1440P, making this device a perfect fit for work-from-home professionals and high-end video gaming.
Key Features
- Supports DisplayPort, DVI, & VGA with approved adapters.  - Keyboard passthrough mode allows for better compatibility plus multimedia key functions  - Hotkey triggers assignable to either Scroll Lock or Right-CTRL  - USB sharing works with printers, jump drives, USB headsets and webcams for teleconferencing.  - Auto-scanning with adjustable time interval from 1 to 250 seconds.  - 1-year US-based warranty when purchased from BuyTESmart
FAQ
- Audio is transmitted via HDMI connection. HDMI or DisplayPort monitor required for audio  - Use powered USB hub to expand the USB2.0 port if multiple connections are needed  - Use USB extender to reduce distance to receiver if wireless mouse/keyboard is “jumpy”  - Please install CR2025 battery before use (battery not included)
Mouse / Keyboard Compatibility
Dedicated mouse & keyboard ports have emulation programming. Second gen chipsets have “Passthrough Mode” to send special characters such as multimedia keys (play, pause, volume up/down), which improves keyboard compatibility. Keyboard software such as Logitech Options, Corsair iCUE, etc conflicts with the emulation and limits the ability to use macro keys. We recommend using the KVM’s USB2.0 port as a workaround if this software if needed. USB2.0 port does not have emulation programming and KVM will not recognize hotkey commands. Please use a powered USB hub to expand USB connectivity.
features: Share 4 computers with 1 monitor, keyboard, mouse. Switch between PC’s with push button, keyboard hotkeys, mouse gestures, or remote control. Can also use as HDMI switch for gaming consoles and streaming devices.
HDMI 2.0 supports UHD 4K60hz (3840x2160), QHD 144hz (2560x1440), 1080P 144hz (1920x1080). Plus Ultrawide 60hz (21:9 & 32:9). HDCP 2.2, HDR10, Dolby Vision. EDID Emulation.
Plug & Play. No software or drivers needed. Supported by IT departments for work-from-home professionals. Separate your work from your personal life without the hassle & clutter!
Transmit audio to monitor via HDMI and/or external speakers via stereo output. Share USB device such as webcam and headset with dedicated USB2.0 port. Great for Zoom and Twitch users.
Supports wired, wireless, mechanical, multimedia, and gaming keyboards. No delay, zero latency keyboard switching and pass-through mode for multimedia keys. READ FAQ & PICS.
U.S. Based TECHNICAL SUPPORT from BuyTESmart available to help resolve all issues.

reviews.title: Mostly good
reviews.text: One of my connected PCs has a hard time getting the resolution to 1440, cannot figure out why.  I have to restart to get it to go from 1080 to 1440.

reviews.title: Great KVM switch
reviews.text: I wasn’t sure what to expect , but after having it for a week I really like the switch.  Perfect size and functionality.

title: Akko Pink Keycaps Set, Clear Translucent 155-Key ASA Profile Keys with Mac Keycaps Set for Mechanical Keyboard
features: TRANSPARENT KEYCAPS – Deliver premium and shining RGB experiences with Akko ASA Clear keycap sets! Made by polycarbonate, the keycap is solid and translucent so that your switches and RGB array can shine through. Characters on the ASA Clear keycaps are processed through UV printing with wear-resistant and scratch-resistant tests, and are not easily wiped off.

ULTIMATE CUSTOMIZATION FLEXIBILITY – Comes with Akko ASA profile, the 155-key keycap set is suitable for a variety of layouts including 61-key(60%), 64-key, 68-key (65%), 84-key (75%), 87-key (TKL), 98-Key (Classic 1800), 100-Key (96%), Alice and full 108-key size, and thus offers strong flexibility for keyboard personalization.

MAC KEYCAPS & NOVELTY KEYS – The keycap set is designed for both Windows and Mac users, where Mac modifiers CMD/OPT are offered in different sizes. The transparent keycap set also features extra cute animal themed novelty keys for decoration.

ASA PROFILE – Akko’s proprietary ASA profile is tailored to offer a more familiar and comfortable typing experience by matching the height for OEM profile while maintaining the aesthetics and spheric top of the classic SA profile.

FOR MX STRUCTURE SWITCHES – Compatible with MX structure mechanical switches and mainstream keyboards including but not limited to Cherry, Gateron, Akko and Kailh. Large keys of non-standard layout & stabilizers may not be compatible.

reviews.title: It feels so nice to type!
reviews.text: I really like the feel of the glossy keys. I wish more keycaps came with the gloss feel like these. RBG lights look awesome! I want more!!! :)

reviews.title: It feels so nice to type!
reviews.text: I really like the feel of the glossy keys. I wish more keycaps came with the gloss feel like these. RBG lights look awesome! I want more!!! :)

reviews.title: Impressed
reviews.text: Very good packaging and acceptable quality

reviews.title: Unique keys look cool with RGB. Some drawbacks.
reviews.text:
Pros:
- Beautifully and conveniently packaged and organized. Caps come on three trays with plastic film on them so that nothing will have shifted in transit. It's very easy to replace your caps and keep your old set save and organized in the trays, too. Simple to find any specialized keys that you may need in the trays.
- Print is easy to see and seems durable/high contrast. No funky fonts that are hard to read.
- Looks awesome with RGB.

Cons:

- There are no tactile nubs on the F and J keys to indicate homerow. As a touch typist, this was almost a dealbreaker.
- Color is way off from the images in the description. The images in the description show a clear white color to the keycaps. Along with the "clear/translucent" descriptors, I believed I was going to get more of a white/iced translucent look. Nope. The caps are basically colorless clear. They are smooth and glossy with no texture. I left some of the OG white keys on my keyboard to show the color clearly side by side. Not the look I was expecting from the images and description.

Good to know:

- I've never used ASA profile before, and generally prefer XDA, so these are a little taller than I'm used to on XDA, but more rounded (and, I think, more comfortable, than OEM. I had some lousy stock OEM caps on my old keyboard, and I do feel these caps are an improvement. They are sufficiently dense, and give me a slightly deeper, more muted sound from my keypresses with my brown switches, which I like, compared to the more plasticky/hollow caps that were original to my board.
- Despite having a glossy finish, I don't find these keys to be slippery--the slight curvature leads your fingertips toward the center of the keys easily and they're comfortable to use.
- I really hate that there are no homerow nubs. Who skips that? Come on.

reviews.title: No tactile bumps on F and J, amazing for backlighting
reviews.text:
I have the “White” color option in this set of keycaps from Akko. White is a bit misleading, the color is actually glossy and nearly translucent. This is actually what I wanted, I was looking all over for nearly clear keycaps, but I did want to point that out.

If you like maximum backlighting, this might be the set for you. I was using white pudding caps before, and I thought they let a lot of light shine through. The difference is night and day. These Akko caps show backlighting on all sides and the top of the cap. I’ve included a picture of a white pudding cap surrounded by the clear Akkos to show the difference.

I’m using the keycaps with Akko Vintage White (MX) switches. The keycaps fit well, but they took a bit of force to fit on the switches.

I am disappointed the F and J keys do not have ridges or bumps. I’m a touch typist and using an ergo board with 56 keys, I rely on those ridges to help me orient my hands. This set has so much variety that I wish Akko had included doubles of F and J or even blanks with the ridges.

Speaking of keycap variety: the set has some great 1u options! My current keyboard is an Iris Rev 6, all keys are 1u size. But most keycap sets don’t have 1u keycaps for Shift, Control, Command/Windows, and so on. This set does, which is a big help. I also like the cute animal keycaps, they add some fun to my board.

I believe the ASA height is original to Akko. They have a helpful height chart on their site, comparing ASA to OEM, JDA, OSA, and Cherry. I was using OEM before, and switching to ASA and the glossy feel of the keycaps took about two days of adjustment.

title: nuphy Air60 Portable Wireless Keyboard, 60% Low Profile Mechanical Keyboard, Supports Bluetooth 5.0, 2.4G and Wired Connection, Compatible with Windows and Mac OS Systems-Gateron Brown Switch
features: ✨【The new upgrade】：NuType F1 is highly praised by many users. On this basis, we have upgraded NuType F1 in all aspects and got the latest Air60 low-shaft mechanical keyboard. It adopts 60% layout (64 keys), bright color scheme, and the design of low shaft will not hurt your wrist after using the keyboard for a long time. The wide keycap design can help reduce the number of keystroke errors.

💻【Three-mode design】：Each user's usage is different. Based on user needs, we try to adapt the Air60 to as many users as possible, so the Air60 keyboard supports 2.4G, Bluetooth wireless connection and type C wired connection, and can connect up to 4 equipments at the same time to meet more complex user needs.

💪【Alternative laptop keyboard】：The four foot pads on the back of the Air60 can be perfectly embedded between the keys of the notebook keyboard. Typing on the Air60 will not touch the notebook keyboard at the bottom, giving notebook users a better typing experience. It is compatible with most laptops on the market, including the latest Macbooks.

💡【60% Portable Keyboard】：Compared with Air75, Air60 has no F area and will be smaller in size. If you don’t use F area often, Air60 is undoubtedly a better choice for use on the go; if you often use a laptop, Air60 can be perfectly placed. On the keyboard of the notebook computer, it is currently suitable for the vast majority of computers on the market, especially the Mac series, and it is more convenient to carry.

🔋【2500mAh battery】：The battery size of the Air60 wireless keyboard is 2500 mAh. The laboratory test time is more than 48 hours, which can meet the needs of a week of office work. When the backlight mode is turned off, the use time can be greatly increased.

reviews.title: Works great. Battery life is pathetic
reviews.text: Keyboard works great. Compact. Keys are responsible with good travel. The battery, however, lasts just about a day, which is unacceptable.

reviews.title: Didn't think low profile could feel this good...
reviews.text:
I was hesitant to try out this keyboard because I have always avoided low profile mechanical keyboards.

Maybe because I did actually type very well and fast with the chic-lit style keyboards that this keyboard reminds me of and I am too deep into the mechanical keyboard hobby to really want to move to low profile.

I am glad I took the leap!

I really like the way the browns feel on this keyboard. It is a pleasure to type on and the space bars and other stabilized keys feel great. Out of the box I don't think there is anything you need to do to this keyboard except use it!

Some things will take getting use to like the smaller shift button. I actually wouldn't mind if this board was spread out a bit more to allow for more traditional length buttons, but I'm sure it is something I could pick up quickly.

The build quality feels excellent and the polished steel plate on the bottom is a great touch. It really looks classy and I love the look of this keyboard. Nuphy's color-ways have always been super slick to me and this is no different.

The board has awesome RGB effects that you can change easily from the board itself. It connects wired, through bluetooth, or through a 2.4ghz adapter. Bluetooth connected instantly and works super well.

It comes with a ton of accessories, from magnetic feet to tilt the board a bit to extra keycaps, pullers, switches, stickers, and the instructions are also a poster of an anime chick which would probably look good in a gamer style room.

I am really tempted to try and use this board as a daily driver but right now I'm using it with it sitting on my M1 Macbook Airs keyboard and it is working great for that use case. It is thin enough that it doesn't block the screen or anything like that.

I have another compact 60% board that has the same goals as this Nuphy board and it misses the mark. Nuphy really hit the nail on the head with this board.

title: DROP + Invyr Holy Panda Mechanical Keyboard Switches - Plate Mounted Tactile Switches, Cherry MX Style, 67g Spring, by Kailh (70 PCS)
features: THE MOST TACTILE SWITCHES IN THE WORLD: With a name like Holy Panda, these switches had to be epic. Made with stems from Halo switches, housing found on Invyr Panda switches, Holy Panda switches combine the best of both worlds.
THE BEST OF BOTH WORLDS: The result is a snappy, tactile switch that is satisfying to type on.

COMPATIBILITY: Holy Panda switches are compatible with plate-mounted Cherry-style PCB's and are perfect for hot-swap compatible barebones kits such as the Drop ALT, CTRL, SHIFT, ENTR, Carina, Planck, and Preonic.
SPECS: Halo Switch Stems, Polycarbonate top housing, Nylon bottom housing, 67g spring, Plate Mount, and MX-compatible.

reviews.title: Excellent tactile
reviews.text:
I have purchased these, lubed Glorious Panda, and Tecsee Purple panda.

This is the best with the other 2 being very close to as good as this. The tactile-ness is a little better than the other both. With the other 2, the tactile is right at the beginning, while this one starts at the beginning and seems to feel the same until bottoming out. All have the nice bump back.

I didn't lube these and they sound and feel great. I think this is my favorite tactile now. But if you're on budget, I would recommend one of the others.

reviews.title: Not a bad one in the bunch
reviews.text: Got the 90 pack and not a broken or bent pin in the entire bunch. Professionally packed and working 100 percent. All switches tested and working fine. A little expensive but worth it for the fast Amazon delivery.

title: NPKC Blank Thick PBT OEM Profile 108 ANSI Keycaps for MX Switches Mechanical Keyboard (Only Keycap) (Dark Blue)
description:

reviews.text: I purchased these for my Das Keyboard Model S (Mac). All of the keys fit, including the spacebar, and they are much higher quality than the Das OEM keys. They feel more substantial, and the sound of the keyboard is a little different, the clacking is lower pitched.

reviews.title: Really looks nice!
reviews.text: Looks really nice, not really that great at allowing RGB to shine through but overall I think it's a nice, beautiful addition to any keyboard.  Mine fit perfectly and adds a certain nice uniqueness to a keyboard that I, as an artist, really enjoy.  I'd definitely recommend, this is really nice looking.

reviews.title: Really Nice Artisan, But May Fit Tightly
reviews.text: The quality of this artisan is really good for the price, and it looks quite nice when illuminated by backlighting as well. The keycap does fit quite tightly but I did manage to get it to fit though some may have issues with fitment. Overall it's a nice artisan but may fit tightly so be aware of that when ordering.

title: Sumgsn 172 Keys Double Shot Apollo Keycaps Cherry Profile for 61/64/87/104/108 Cherry Mx Switches ISO/ANSI Layout Mechanical Keyboard
features: Production Methods-These keycaps are made of double shot craft, with good texture and clear legends, with a thickness of approximately 1.5mm, and will not be backlit.c
Wide Compatibility-It has a total of 172 keys, including various sizes of keycaps suitable for most cherry mx switches 60% 65% 95% ISO/ANSI layout mechanical keyboards.
Profile-These keycaps are ergonomically designed Cherry profile keycaps that can provide you with a comfortable typing experience, better for work and gaming.
Packaing-This product is packaged with ABS keycaps tray with tray cover and comes with a key extractor, which can effectively protect the keycaps during transportation and avoid scratches and stains.
Warm Tips-Only keycaps, not a keyboard. if the keycaps missing or broken, please contact customer service to send you new keys.

reviews.title: Great set
reviews.text: Bought these for a budget build, my set was perfect. Quality exceeded expectations.

reviews.title: Spectacular!
reviews.text:
all i have to say is, get these keycaps. i was truly surprised at the quality of these keycaps. they feel really nice, solid, and with a little grain on the top that are very comfortable. Pricewise i think these are right on. At about $50 the quality is really good without the wait and the much higher prices of some other premium ABS...the colorway is one that i really like. I have always had a handful of colorways i wanted, the botonical, these Hammerhead, modern dolch, etc. the legends are pretty consistent with a few fonts thinner than others but i count 2 keys in my 60% board. not bad and i don't really notice it. i really like the color scheme more than i would notice the font issues.

Now i only wish i would have have a bigger board, 75% or TKL to take advantage of the other keys. geez keyboard world you will bankrupt me.

Very beautiful set. i'm coming back to my 60% for a little while.

reviews.title: Lovely colors, plenty of options!
reviews.text: Key caps look really great. Tons of combinations of caps for just about every layout. I threw it on an old WYSE WY-60 keyboard and it had some uncommon unit sizes for a few keys. Worked out great after finding keycaps that fit its spot. I had to grind down the edges of a few keys to fit them nicely. A modern keyboard will have no compatibility issues without a doubt!

reviews.title: Perfect
reviews.text: Although it was pricey I loved the color scheme and wanted it. It has extra bits like longer or shorter space bar and ctrl, alt keys in case your board needs them.

reviews.title: Quality keycaps
reviews.text:
Well built and produced keycaps. This keyboard includes keys for Aifei, not sure if they are the ones who made these keycaps or the ones they are cloning.

Very nice and bright colors, font looks great.

Though this set seems to have a bit of lower profile and standard keycaps. So just a heads up on that.

Overall I am happy with these Keycaps.

reviews.title: Can be used stock!
reviews.text:
[[VIDEOID:528307786b36a4c2c9833ddd389101cb]] They come with a small amount of lube pre applied. Usually, prelubed switches are inconsistent in my experience but these ones are really good. I decided to not lube them at all. EDIT: got a few more batches, they're not as consistent. But seller was nice enough to get them replaced.

The stems are long pole. As a result, it has a nice bottom out sound.

It was noticeably heavier coming from a 50g switch to these that are 63g, but overtime I got adjusted and had no problems typing with them.

Overall a great buy, though I wish it was priced more competitively.

Using on Drop Preonic with MT3 Camillo, plateless, tape mod

reviews.title: Nice linears
reviews.text:
Starting off not sure who's at fault for this as it's only fulfilled by Amazon but one of my bags was missing a few switches (one bag was open and all the switches were rattling around inside the amazon pouch it arrived in) I counted 5 missing and there wasn't a silica gel packet either, the other bags were fine.
complaint aside they feel really nice and the factory lube seems to be well done as wish they had a "3 pin" option and different switch amounts 35 2 is too few and 3 is too many (unless some get lost in transit like mine lol) 90 would be a good option for the TKL/75% gang

reviews.title: Will be Using Right Away
reviews.text: In building & repairing computers these key switches are used very often. Mainly for high-end (over $100) keyboards that the serious gamers use. What they do is tough on the keyboards and often switches break or become less responsive which may lead to a gamer losing a match. These snap right in to the sockets and can instantly rebuild the whole keyboard like new. I realize they would not be used by your everyday person or casual gamer but when it gets serious they are  the switches to be used.

reviews.title: Nice Linear
reviews.text: These are nice and smooth and sound wonderful.  I'm not a linear fan usually, but I do like them in my modifier keys (shift, backspace, enter, and space) because I don't like the return sound a tactile makes while in a larger key.  These are perfect for that as they don't have a high pitch and there's no spring ping to even think of!  I would highly recommend them to anyone looking for a lighter-weight linear switch with some style.

reviews.title: Great Stock!
reviews.text: The switches sounds great and feels good to use. The lube in this is not bad at all and you don't hear any ping or scratchiness. The bottom out is much more than I am used to, but I do enjoy it otherwise.

title: Kraken Coiled Keyboard Cable - Artisan Coiled USB C Cable with Aviator Connector for Mechanical Gaming Keyboards (Pure White)
features: Premium Artisan Designs in colors to fit every gaming setup!
Heavy Duty 5 Pin Aviator Connector (Easy to connect, and looks AMAZING)
Double Sleeved for enhanced looks and durability!
5 Feet long to reach your keyboard anywhere in your setup

reviews.title: Excellent quality cable and connectors, but color is more purple than blue
reviews.text:
Update: Oddly enough, this did not work with a Drop Alt keyboard. It worked with a few other keyboards just fine though. Just a word of warning for anyone pairing this cable with a Drop Alt (or maybe any other Drop keyboard).

This is the third coiled cable I have ordered, and it's definitely the best quality overall. The cable itself is thick and good quality. The coil is a bit firm, but nothing too unusual. The intermediate connector is all metal and heavy. Both sides of the intermediate connector can be unthreaded: The side going to the USB-C connector, and the side going to the USB connector.

One interesting thing I noticed was that the end connectors have a color matched almost shrink wrap thickness strain protection around them. The USB side is the most noticeable, and the USB-C side is a bit thicker. As long as there is enough strain protection, I actually prefer the less bulky end connections.

The only nit is the color. Even though it looked dark purplish in the product listing, I was hoping that it would be more blue in real life. It's not, it's an attractive dark purple. I would assume the color represented in the product listing is accurate, in case you're wondering if there is any color variation from the listing photos to real life.

title: DROP + Matt3o MT3 Susuwatari Custom Keycap Set, ABS Hi-Profile Keycaps, Doubleshot Legends, Cherry MX Compatible Set for Ortholinear Mechanical Keyboards (Ortholinear)
features: A GROUNDBREAKING PROFILE, BACK FOR MORE: Sculpted in Hi-Profile, Matt3o’s groundbreaking MT3 /dev/tty custom keycap set changed the way we type.

FROM THE IBM TERMINAL TO THE DESKTOP: Based on the profile form the iconic IBM beamspring terminals of the 1970s, this custom set is fully sculpted in Hi-Profile. From its heavily slanted design to the scooped, contoured tops of each cap, the set recreates the feel of vintage keyboards.

DOUBLESHOT LEGENDS: As opposed to a dye-sublimiation process, these doubleshot legends are permanently molded inside the keycap, thus becoming an integral part of it. This ensures reliable legends and continuity throughout the set.
ORTHOLINEAR: For ortho keyboards like the Drop Planck, Preonic, and other boards with similar layouts.
SPECS: Produced by Drop and designed by Matt3o, ABS Hi-Profile MT3 keycaps, Doubleshot legends, MX compatible, and Fully sculpted based on IBM beamspring keycaps.

reviews.title: Absolute necessity on ortholinear boards
reviews.text:
These keycaps are thick, double shot so they will never fade, and sculpted. With ortholinear keyboards, these keycaps really help adjusting to smaller modifer key size and different key layout.

Pros:
1. Thick: This helps lower the pitch of the sound, which makes it sound quieter.
2. Doubleshot ABS: No wearing out the legends. The font is also fairly thick, making it much more legible than some other keycaps.
3. MT3 profile: The sculpted profile really makes it very comfortable to use and helps reduce hitting the wrong keys. No sharp edges due to the spherical shape. Very enjoyable for typing. The profile is much higher though so that could be an issue for some people.
4. Multiple profile options for certain keys: For example, the Ctrl and Esc keycs come in profiles that would allow it to be the top row or 3rd row. Space bar comes as two 1u, two 1.5u, and one 2u.
5. Storage tray and box: This seems silly to put as a pro but the tray and box it comes with make it easy to store unused or keycaps it replaced. Other Keycaps come in a zip lock bag that will just be a jumbled mess.

Cons:
1. Price. This is really expensive for a set of keycaps but this is the normal price for any specialty keycaps. It is what it is.
2. Limitation of scultped profile: Putting key profiles meant for row 1 in row 3 feels awkward so you might end you designing your keymap partially based on keycap profiles. Also might end up rotating some keycaps like row 5 Raise or Lower layer keycaps to make it more comfortable.
3. ABS option only. The PBT version is in white only. So if you prefer the color with the material that it isn't made it, then you're out of luck.
4. Limited to buying sets if you need extra keys: If you need more keys, you have to order a set. I would like to have more 1.5u blank keycaps but don't want to buy multiple sets just for the few pieces I want.

reviews.title: I like them
reviews.text:
These are my daily drivers, I use them on a Drop CTRL with Glorious Panda switches. Initially I thought the MT3 profile was disorienting, but after using them for a while, I am quite fond of it. I acknowledge that not everyone will like this profile though, and that should be considered if you are buying this product (or any like it). If you can find an MT3 set to try first before dumping about two hundred dollars on keycaps, it would be a **very good** idea. Other things go without saying, the thickness is very good the weight on my switches feels good and not like its making keys too easy to press or anything. I really like the theme, but all of that considered, I bought this specifically for the VI keys (hjkl with arrows) because I thought they looked super cool.

The price for these keycaps is a good deterrent for most people, for good reason. However this high of a price is pretty standard for high quality keycaps, as well as the Drop.com-exclusive MT3 profile.

This leads me to the value for money, its expensive and definitely should only be bought if you have a hankering to spend money on some expensive keycaps or you really really like having a specific row of keycaps.
Brightness is a 1 star because they're not shine-through keycaps, as they should be.
Gaming is a 4 star because, like, Sure? nothing about these keycaps keep you from gaming.

reviews.title: Best 3rd party caps I've ever owned.
reviews.text:
I really liked the esthetics of these key caps because I was looking for something which resembled "Old Nasa" feel, and these were the best I could find. The ergonomics were also great and everything has a very nice and snug fit, unlike the original keycaps that came with my CTRL keyboard.

I only wish that the "additional" buttons came with the normal key caps for the money I spent on the original "package". $30 for about 10 extra keys I can use on my ten keyless is VERY steep.

reviews.title: Best keycap set
reviews.text: I love this set. Worth every penny.

reviews.title: Absolutely gorgeous
reviews.text: Wonderful keycaps , goes great on my Nyquist Keyboard, looks professional yet custom, I highly recommend.

title: HONKID Esc Keycap for Mechanical Gaming Keyboard Switches, Custom Keycaps for Esc and Tab Key with Magnetic Light Macintosh Retro Style Design (White)
features: TRIBUTE TO THE CLASSICS: The retro computer keycaps is in honor of the the classic 1984 Macintosh computers, the unique retro styling of ESC keycap and Tab keycap give you a new experience

COMPATIBILITY: Uinversal mechanical keyboard Cross interface, compatible with most MX switches as cherry MX, kailh, outemu, gateron, ect. Our custom keycaps fit most keyboards especially 60% mechanical gaming keyboard, the keycaps include ESC keycap and Tab keycap

LIGHT-TRANSMISSION DESIGN: Unique light guide design, the light transmission is brighter and more uniform. If your keyboard has a backlight function, then your light will be delivered through the keycap card, and the effect is perfect. Five classic card patterns, which can be changed at will

MAGNETIC DESIGN: Both ESC and Tab keycap have magnetic, simple and stable.You can attach the mini keyboard to any keycap, card panel of the keycap also uses the magnetic function, which makes it very convenient and fast to replace the translucent cards
DELICATE EXQUISITE: You will feels better and more delicate and comfortable,The delicate matte texture and the details of the back are enough to make you miss the first generation of MAC

reviews.title: Perfect starter Artisan keycap!
reviews.text:
Edit: purchased the black AND the white one. 10/10

I purchased this keycap as I wanted an artisan but did not want to have to pay resale on ebay or Mechmarket. After a quick search, I was glad to find this combo as it covers the escape key AND the tab key in a matching set. I tried both keycaps on multiple boards and I am thoroughly impressed.

I can easily say that this keycap set will fit both regular MX stems and box stems, as they fit on my box inks and NK creams without issue. I swapped them on 6 different boards just to see how they looked with different sets and did not find there to be any loosening.

Being able to change the "screen" is a fun touch but I am leaving it on the apple logo for nostalgia. The keyboard on the tab key is magnetic and I have found myself playing with it while working today.

Overall this is an easy 10/10 for both price and quality. I highly recommend it for both enthusiasts who want a fun budget set but also for anyone with a keyboard in general!

title: Hexgears Impulse - Hot-swap Mechanical Keyboard (Box Brown Switches, Pudding Keycaps)
description:
The Hexgears Impulse is available in many amazing configurations.
— Durable PBT Keycaps
PBT is an engineering plastic that resists shine and finger grease. This is far better than the cheap plastic used by major brands. All Impulses use PBT in their doubleshot injection molded keycaps — the main letters won’t ever wear off.
— Awesome RGB Backlighting and RGB Ring Lighting
The Impulse lighting system is on-board, ensuring compatibility with various operating systems. Translucent pudding keycaps showcase its smooth RGB lighting in a way that other keyboards can only dream of. RGB customization options allow you to select an experience that matches your battlestation.
— Survives Your Worst Impulses
Do you have a coffee habit? A cat that spills things on the regular? The Impulse handles liquids like a champ. IP56 water resistance means that the keyboard functions while blasted with water jets. Its curved metal frame is also quite rigid.
— Kailh Box Switches
Kailh Box switches are rated for 80 million presses — way above industry standard. They have drainage ports and self-cleaning housings. Box switches also feel excellent, as they’re less wobbly and noticeably smoother than standard models. Tactile Box switches result in relatively quiet mechanical keyboards, but the clicky versions go all out with a new and improved mechanism.
— Other Features
The Impulse is designed as a plug and play device. Its feature set offers exactly what you need to get up and running fast. 2-stage angle adjustment feet and basic onboard functions like Windows key locking enhance your experience.
Impulse keyboards have a 1-year limited warranty.
features: RGB, Per-key customizable lighting modes, RGB Light Ring
Two-shot extra durable PBT keycaps (Pudding & 2-tone)
Curved metal mounting plate with 2 stage adjustable height feet
IP56 water resistant design, N-key Rollover (NKRO), integrated USB-A cable
Available in Kailh BOX Brown (Tactile), BOX White (Clicky), and Hako Clear (Heavy Tactile) Mechanical Switches
Updated Impulse V2 design with Hot-swap switch sockets (MX-compatible, LED compatible, plate mount)

reviews.title: Good Keyboard, clicky, and positive return
reviews.text:
Hi, I am a bit of a keyboard junky. I have bought probably 10 mechanical keyboards over the last 3 years. I still have an original IBM PC keyboard - that is really stiff and loud. But about this keyboard, I read an online review and said, why not - and I have been using it for several days. First, it sets up easy. Just plug in to get started. Configuring all the lights is more work and not completely intuitive - especially if you lose the little manual 3x4" or so. Personally, I don't care that much for the lighting features on any keyboard - I want to know if I can type quickly without any errors from the keyboard as opposed from me. In general, this keyboard is one of the best. The key depression effort is light to medium - if you rest your fingers on the keys, they will not trigger -which is good. The space bar works well - and I usually find this a problem because I hit it a bit off center - but this one works well. The noise is medium. The clacky sound is the sound of me pressing the keys to the maximum and not the click of the membrane.

I generally like this keyboard and I have tried many from $79 mechanical keys to $200 keyboards. I think this is up there with the best - maybe not all the bells and whistles like volume control, but I never use that. Buy this. Works well. Feels good under the fingers. Type fast!

reviews.title: Recommended for Gaming
reviews.text: Nice keyboard. Has NKRO, macro support, and lots of lighting patterns for the keyboard and ring lighting. Looks very nice in the Pudding keycaps version with full RGB. I ordered it with the Kailh Box Brown switches, which work smoothly, and have a somewhat subtle tactile feedback. I tested it fully, and there was no key chatter detected. Well worth the reasonable asking price and one of the very few current options offering these switches in a commercial build.

reviews.title: Great for typing
reviews.text:
I have the version with white keycaps and "box white" clicky switches. I don't play computer games, so I can't comment on that aspect of the keyboard. But I can say that this is a very good typist's keyboard. The key switches sound like Cherry Blues, but I think these feel smoother, and have less lateral wiggle room.

The keyboard comes with a tiny "user guide" that is no more than a list of keystrokes that control the lighting and macros. I don't care for lighting on a keyboard, so the first thing I tried to do was dim the lights. The "user guide" wasn't very clear on that, so here's how:

While holding down the "Fn" key...
... press the Down arrow to dim the white lights.
... press the "2" key on the numeric keypad to dim the colored perimeter lights.

Keep pressing those keys to dim the lights further. I've now got them completely dimmed, and I hope they stay that way.

One thing to know about this keyboard is that the keycaps aren't recessed. On most keyboards, you can't see or grasp the bottoms of the keycaps, because they fit into cavities formed by the molded plastic deck. This keyboard's top deck is flat metal, without cavities. So there's about a quarter-inch gap between the keycap bottoms and the top of the deck. As a result, it's easy to pull the keycaps. You can even remove the Escape key with your bare fingers. I don't think this is a bad thing; it's just worth knowing about.

Speaking of keycaps, these use a stylized font that sacrifices legibility for fashion. The designer apparently dislikes closed shapes, and created little vertical gaps in the Q, O, zero, 8, etc. Not even the ampersand, pound sign, or the little orbs of the percent sign are spared this annoying treatment. It looks like the dye is flaking away. As a sometimes hunt-and-peck typist, I find this very distracting, as it keeps me from instantly recognizing many of the keys.

I would rate this keyboard with five stars, if not for the inadequate "user guide" and the poorly thought-out keycap font.

reviews.title: Very well built
reviews.text: Solid keys, good feel.  I don't need all of the LEDS, and had to play around with the controls to get just the minimum lighting.  Much better than my old "mushy" wireless keyboard.

reviews.title: Good quality
reviews.text: Good quality and even better sound. Slightly pricey for the quantity but very much worth it.

title: Lomiluskr K680T Wireless 60% Mechanical Keyboard, Ultra-Compact Bluetooth/Wired Dual Mode Keyboard, White Backlight, Compatible for Multi-Device Connection (Black Switch, Black)
features: 【68 Key Mini and Compact Layout】60% Layout 68Keys Mechanical Keyboard. As a compact mechanical keyboard, while minimize the size to 13.4×4.25×1.4inch, we maintain the Mac key position and commonly used keys for games. This size saves lots of desktop space, giving your right hand and mouse more space, whether you are working, gaming or daily use, it will perform perfectly. Small in the appearance but big inside

【Bluetooth Wireless/Wired Mode】Designed with Bluetooth mode and wired dual mode, you have more choices. Bluetooth3.0 is stable and strong enough to ensure 10 meters transmission distance. It can connect up to 3 devices at the same time, simply press FN+Q/W/E to exchange between devices, easy to use and suitable for both work and game. Moreover, the wired mode will also charge the keyboard at the same time, a cool function and time-saver indeed

【1000mAh Built-in Large Capacity Battery】N-Key Rollover and Built-in 1000mAh Battery. This model is programmed to be N-key rollover in wired mode. The keys won’t conflict with each other. Save you from trouble while playing a game. Built-in large capacity battery ensures long time use without falling behind

【18 Light Effects】The letters are made to shine-through if the backlight is on. Simple backlight but you have got up to 18 light effects, classic and neat (flashing, static or breathing, etc.) use FN+→/←/↑/↓ to choose the ideal mode, freely adjust the brightness and light speed.

【Professional Mechanical Switches】Mechanical Switches and Professional Chip. This model is equipped with the custom chip for fast and lossless response. The 68 keys are using the real mechanical switches for ultimate tapping experience. Lightweight, weak tactile and audible feedback, these switches are the perfect combination of typing and gaming, more than 50 million click tests for excellent durability.

reviews.title: Won't pair with M1 Macbook Air, works well with Dell XPS 9370 and Acer Triton Predator
reviews.text:
For windows users this keyboard is great. The brown switches feel somewhat similar to higher end cherry brown switches and I had no complaints with the build quality. There are various lighting modes (my favorite is the single keystroke light trail).

I was slightly disappointed with the Bluetooth not working on my M1 Macbook Air but I had no other issues with various windows laptops. There is some latency noticeable when waking from sleep but it's not too big of a deal. For gamers it might be though so you'll want to use it wired.

Overall it's a good deal for a mechanical 60% bt keyboard as long as you're not expecting premium switches or bt 5.0 connectivity.

reviews.title: Compact But Has Good Weight, Keys Are Perfect
reviews.text:
I opted for the "brown" keys and I must say that they feel and respond perfectly. Just the right amount of "clack" and just the right amount of resistance. The keys are normal width but the footprint is compact so you don't have to sacrifice any typing comfort for the portability or the ability to work in small spaces. The keyboard itself is very weighty and between its heft and rubber feet it's pleasingly very stable - won't slide around on the desk. The ability to use either USB or Bluetooth makes this thing incredibly versatile. Thankfully it's USB-C so you don't have to worry about plug alignment, and the included USB cable is very good quality (braided nylon, strain relief) and length. The backlighting is decent, though not terribly even so some of the lettering doesn't look right in the dark.

One big downside is the lack of dedicated Function keys (F1-F12). This keyboard takes a cue from laptop keyboard design and introduces an "Fn" key which you have to hold in to change the function of normal keys to specialty operations, but instead of controlling volume or screen brightness, the "Fn" key is required to activate bluetooth pairing, an feature that acts sort of like Caps Lock but for function keys and of course the "F" keys. The problem I ran into with this configuration is that the F1-F12 keys weren't being recognized with some devices. I actually bought this specifically to use with a MiSTer FPGA device which supports standard USB keyboards and requires the F keys for certain basic functions.

According to the instructions, the combination "Fn + 1" should look like an "F1" key press but it did not. After poking at it for some time I discovered an undocumented (as in it's not in the instructions anywhere) feature which subtly changes the keyboard's operation. The "A" and "S" keys have an additional label "Os1" and "Os2" respectively. Holding "Fn + A" or "Fn + S" makes the keyboard backlight flash (which it does whenever you've changed some function of the keyboard itself). With the keyboard set to "Os2" (Fn + S) the "Fn + 1" combination DOES send "F1" but, this arrangement also confusingly swaps the "Window" key and the left "Alt" key.

Bottom Line ---
Pros:
- Heavy
- Quality keys, great feel
- Bluetooth & USB
- USB is USB-C and comes with a good cord
- Easy to plug in and use

Neutral Observations:
- Backlight is uneven

Cons:
- Instructions are incomplete
- Not having dedicated F1-F12 keys is problematic for gaming
